name: tennis
description: stylish CSV tables in your terminal
readme_summary: Tennis is a command-line interface tool written in Zig that displays CSV data in a stylish, well-formatted table directly within the terminal. It automatically adjusts to terminal size, applies color themes based on background, and formats numbers for readability.
key_features: auto-layout to fit your terminal window
auto-themes to pick light or dark based on your terminal background
auto-detect csv vs tsv (or semis, or pipes)
primary_use_case: Displaying CSV data in a formatted, readable table within the terminal.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled craftsman meticulously arranging colorful, polished wooden blocks into a neat, organized table structure within a cozy, dimly lit workshop. Each block represents a piece of data from a scattered pile of raw, unrefined logs (representing raw CSV data) on one side. The craftsman's tools are subtle digital interfaces, and glowing lines of code flow from a nearby terminal screen, guiding the arrangement. The final table is perfectly aligned and aesthetically pleasing, with subtle highlights indicating its readability. The overall mood is one of order and transformation, showcasing the conversion of messy data into a beautiful, structured output. Style: 3D isometric illustration with warm, inviting lighting.

name: nerd-fonts
description: Iconic font aggregator, collection, & patcher. 3,600+ icons, 50+ patched fonts: Hack, Source Code Pro, more. Glyph collections: Font Awesome, Material Design Icons, Octicons, & more
readme_summary: Nerd Fonts is a project that enhances programming fonts by patching them with a large collection of glyphs and icons from popular iconic font libraries. It provides a font patcher tool for users to customize their own fonts, offering a wide variety of installation options across different operating systems.
key_features: Patches developer-targeted fonts with a high number of glyphs (icons)
Includes glyphs from Font Awesome, Devicons, Octicons, and others
Offers multiple installation options for various operating systems (macOS, Windows, Linux)
Includes over 3,600 icons and 50+ patched fonts
primary_use_case: To provide developers with programming fonts enhanced with a comprehensive set of icons and glyphs, improving the visual representation of code and terminal output.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, magical workshop where a friendly artisan is carefully crafting and embedding glowing, colorful icons into plain, monochrome programming fonts. The artisan uses a special tool that looks like a futuristic chisel, emitting sparks of digital light as it works. On one side, stacks of plain font files are waiting, and on the other, beautifully illustrated, icon-rich fonts are neatly arranged. Small, stylized code snippets float in the air like blueprints. The overall mood is creative and industrious, with warm, inviting lighting. The art style should be a vibrant 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing the transformation and enhancement process.

name: PhotoGIMP
description: A Patch for GIMP 3+ for Photoshop Users
readme_summary: PhotoGIMP is a patch for GIMP that modifies its user interface and keyboard shortcuts to closely resemble Adobe Photoshop. It aims to provide a familiar environment for users transitioning from Photoshop to the free and open-source GIMP, enhancing usability for those accustomed to Adobe's workflow.
primary_use_case: To make GIMP's user interface and controls feel familiar to Adobe Photoshop users, easing the transition for those switching to GIMP.
cover_image_prompt: A split-screen visual metaphor: on one side, a person looks slightly confused and overwhelmed by a complex, unfamiliar toolbox labeled 'GIMP'. On the other side, the same person is now smiling and confidently using a very similar, neatly organized toolbox labeled 'PhotoGIMP', which visually mirrors the layout of a classic Photoshop interface. Subtle digital particles flow from the 'PhotoGIMP' toolbox, indicating a seamless transformation. The background is a clean, modern workspace. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration with clear iconography and a bright, inviting color palette, emphasizing ease of use and familiarity.

name: zerobyte
description: Backup automation for self-hosters. Built on top of restic
readme_summary: Zerobyte is a TypeScript-based backup automation tool designed for self-hosters. It leverages Restic to provide a web interface for scheduling, managing, and monitoring encrypted backups across various storage protocols.
key_features: Automated backups with encryption, compression, and retention policies powered by Restic
Flexible scheduling for automated backup jobs with fine-grained retention policies
Multi-protocol support (NFS, SMB, WebDAV, SFTP, local directories)
primary_use_case: Automating and managing encrypted backups for self-hosted data through a web interface.
cover_image_prompt: A diligent librarian meticulously organizing and protecting ancient, glowing scrolls within a secure, futuristic vault. Each scroll represents a piece of data, and the librarian is shown carefully placing them onto designated shelves, ensuring they are encrypted and compressed. Subtle digital particles flow from the scrolls to a central monitoring console displaying backup status and schedules. The vault is dimly lit, highlighting the preciousness of the data. The repository name 'Zerobyte' is subtly etched on the vault door. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing order, security, and the automated nature of the backup process.

name: server-survival
description: Tower defense game that teaches cloud architecture. Build infrastructure, survive traffic, learn scaling.
readme_summary: Server Survival is an educational 3D simulation game where players act as Cloud Architects. The game teaches cloud infrastructure concepts by challenging players to build, scale, and manage a resilient system to handle traffic loads, defend against attacks, and stay within budget.
key_features: Upgradeable services (API Gateway, Compute, SQL DB, NoSQL DB, Cache)
Multiple traffic types (Static, Read, Write, Upload, Search, Malicious)
primary_use_case: Teaching cloud architecture and DevOps principles through an engaging game simulation.
cover_image_prompt: A miniature, bustling city made of servers and network cables, with a single, determined architect character directing traffic flow. Green and blue data streams representing legitimate traffic are smoothly flowing into the city's infrastructure, while red, spiky 'attack' packets are being deflected by a shimmering shield emanating from a central firewall tower. Small screens around the architect show budget, reputation, and service health meters. The scene is set against a clean, digital background with subtle code snippets and glowing nodes, rendered in a vibrant, 3D isometric illustration style.

name: THE-Process-Manager
description: TUI-based Rust process manager for real-time system monitoring. A privacy-conscious, terminal-based framework leveraging procfs, sysinfo, and crossterm to seamlessly retrieve and display CPU/memory usage, provide advanced process filtering/sorting, and enable on-the-fly process termination.
readme_summary: THE Process Manager is a Rust-based TUI application for real-time system monitoring. It retrieves and displays detailed CPU and memory usage for running processes, offering sorting and filtering capabilities.
primary_use_case: Real-time system monitoring and process management via a terminal user interface.
cover_image_prompt: A vigilant air traffic controller in a futuristic control tower, overseeing a bustling sky filled with distinct aircraft representing running processes. The controller uses a holographic interface to monitor each aircraft's speed (CPU usage) and altitude (memory usage), with clear indicators for each. Some aircraft are highlighted with warning lights, signifying processes that might need attention or termination. Subtle digital readouts and data streams flow around the control tower, hinting at the underlying system. The scene is set during a dynamic twilight, with glowing data lines and a clean, focused composition. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style with vibrant, contrasting colors and high detail.
